name: update_event
description:
Update an existing event in Google Calendar.

Only the provided fields will be modified; omitted fields remain unchanged.

RECURRING EVENTS: When search results show a recurring_event_id, each result is a single occurrence with its own event_id. To modify only ONE occurrence, use that occurrence's event_id. To modify the entire series, use the recurring_event_id (the master event's ID). To change which days a recurring event repeats on, update the recurrence rules on the master event.

Parameters:
- event_id: The event ID to update (required, you may need to search to get it). For recurring events: use the instance event_id to update just that occurrence, or the recurring_event_id to update the whole series.
- summary: Updated event title.
- start_time/end_time: Updated event start and end timestamps. Use YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events, otherwise use RFC3339 timestamps with the user's timezone offset from viewer context (e.g. 1970-01-31T12:00:00-08:00). Always include the offset — never send bare datetimes.
- description: Updated description.
- location: Updated location.
- attendees: Updated attendee list. IMPORTANT: this replaces the entire attendee list. To add or remove a single attendee, first use google_calendar_get_event to get the current attendees, then pass the complete updated list.
- recurrence: Updated recurrence rules (RFC 5545). Only works on master recurring events. Example: ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,SA"] to change a M-W-S event to M-S only.
- timezone: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/Chicago") for the event times.
- send_updates: "all", "externalOnly", or "none" (default). Only set to "all" if the user explicitly asks to notify attendees, or if you believe notifications are important for this update.
- calendar_id: Optional calendar ID: use to update an event on a specific calendar. A calendar_id is in email format: people's email addresses correspond to their primary calendars. Defaults to the user's primary calendar.

event_id.description: The event ID to update (from a previous search result).

start_time.description: Updated start time as RFC3339 with timezone offset (e.g. 1970-01-31T12:00:00-08:00), or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events. Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

end_time.description: Updated end time as RFC3339 with timezone offset (e.g. 1970-01-31T13:00:00-08:00), or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events. Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

attendees.description: Updated attendee list (email addresses). This REPLACES the full attendee list — to add or remove a single attendee, first search for the event to get the current list, then pass the complete updated list.

recurrence.description: Updated recurrence rules (RFC 5545 strings, e.g. ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,SA"]). Only applicable to master recurring events (not individual instances). An empty list means "don't change recurrence".

timezone.description: IANA timezone for the event times (e.g. "America/Chicago").

send_updates.description: Whether to send email notifications to attendees about this update: "all", "externalOnly", or "none" (default). Only set to "all" if the user explicitly asks to notify attendees about the change.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID of the event (e.g. email address from google_calendar_list_calendars). Defaults to the user's primary calendar.

file_url.description: Google Drive file URL (web_view_link from google_drive_search)

attachments.description: Google Drive files to attach to the event. Each entry needs a file_url from google_drive_search. When provided, fully replaces the attachment list. Omit to leave existing attachments unchanged.
